creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to Jeremy Chadwick from comment #2)
> (In reply to Chris Hutchinson from comment #1)
> This is faulty advice.
It may be today. But I was speaking from past experience.
> The sysctl is only available if one's kernel config
> includes "option TCP_RFC7413", which isn't in GENERIC by default because the
> feature is considered experimental (i.e. most FreeBSD boxes will not have
> this option, thus not have the sysctl).  A quick review of stable/11
> sys/conf and amd64/conf also says this is the case there as well, ditto with
> a FreeBSD forum post about this problem.  References for my statement:
As I stated; I was speaking from my past experience with it. I just dredged
up the kernel I was using at that time, and I made no addition regarding
RFC7413. *But* like I said, it was from the past (late 8, and early 9
versions). I'm not trying to argue that you aren't correct. Just that it
appears that at least at *one* time in history, my suggestion was
correct. :)

> 
> * https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base?view=revision&revision=292823
> *
> https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/10/sys/netinet/tcp_fastopen.
> c?revision=292823&view=markup#l27 (read all the way to line 99)
> *
> https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/10/sys/conf/
> files?revision=323223&view=markup#l3580
> *
> https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/stable/10/sys/conf/
> options?revision=322744&view=markup#l450
> * https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/59367/
> * https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/59348/  (faulty advice given at end of
> thread, re: add to /boot/loader.conf)
> 
> Also, I just found Bug 217288 which has been open for the past 9 months, so
> it looks like the one I opened here was a duplicate.  I did not find that
> bug because it lacked the word "fastopen" in the summary.  I will mark this
> as a duplicate.

--Chris
